purpose: define scope, success criteria, boundary, risks with severity, Required Skills per task, and the Readiness Gate in a single session
model_preference: Opus or a model with the highest reasoning quality
preflight: read agents/governance/developer-guidelines.md (§5 rules and review-checklist) and agents/governance/verification-suite.md (architectural gates) before any write
execute kickoff-reads.json > preflight_reconciliation (includes reading status.yaml, invariants, branch/baseline, git reconciliation)
read the scoping-phase artifacts: scope.md and tasks.md (if they exist)
if the feature is new: read the templates feature-scope.template.md and feature-tasks.template.md before creating scope.md/tasks.md
read the governance manifest at the root, the context map, and the canonical domain docs derived from the feature
forbidden_actions: create a tasks/ folder or individual per-task files
edit status.yaml manually with file-writing tools (every edit must go through the manage-status.py script)
emit intermediate chat updates outside transitions.json > communication_contract.allowed_chat_events
violate kickoff-reads.json > read_scope_policy without explicit human authorization
rules: tasks.md is a single file at the feature folder root (no tasks/ folder, no individual files)
Required Skills and Suggested Skill Gaps live EXCLUSIVELY in scope.md

every new or edited Required Skills entry must use a canonical repo-relative path `agents/skills/<skill-name>/skill.md`; a bare name is only accepted as backward compatibility when it resolves to a directory containing an existing skill.md
Success Criteria as an objective checklist for the reviewer
compact Task Index with ID, Goal, Files/Modules and Dependencies
every registered risk declares an explicit severity
Critical Constraints only when specific to this feature
scope.md must contain all mandatory H2 headers (methodology.md §10)
tasks.md must contain the H2 header ## Task Backlog and ### task-XX: [Goal] blocks per task
every ### task-XX block must include an **Objective** bullet with 1 to 5 lines stating the task's problem and motivation; text that merely duplicates Acceptance Criteria is invalid
a complete Readiness Gate is a precondition for the handoff
strict operational silence: chat only on the events allowed by transitions.json > communication_contract
relevant skills are mandatory before use and must be verified for existence in the preflight
do not embed in the scope operational details that belong in skills, prompts, or canonical docs

empirical verification of the repo before declaring (methodology.md §17.4): every path in Files to Modify must carry a (create) or (modify) suffix and be verified against the current worktree via ls/grep/Read before the Readiness Gate; naming conventions, reusable patterns and technology stack also require empirical verification before declaring - personal memory and planning reference docs are no substitute; under ambiguity, stop and ask

closing_actions: run the scope-tasks-consistency script against the feature folder - mechanical cross-artifact gate; exit code !=0 BLOCKS the handoff. Fix scope.md/tasks.md until the script returns ok before calling manage-status.py
update status.yaml by running the script in the terminal: manage-status.py transition-phase implementing --active-artifact tasks.md --active-task <ID of the first pending task>
commit and push the versioned changes within the authorized write scope

closing_output: status.yaml and versioned artifacts committed and pushed. Session closed for resumption by the implementer.
